domain_qualified.feasibility_rationale: Both parties explicitly mentioned openness to a phased trial at T6. Party A needs 'explicit reliability conditions and minimized disruption'; Party B needs assurance it 'clearly leads to meaningful time rather than indefinite delay.' Standard custody transition tool in family mediation. Addresses both parents' core concerns: Party A gets predictability and safety measures, Party B gets defined pathway to expanded role.
conditions: Parties must agree on trial duration (typical range: 4-12 weeks)
Success criteria must be observable and measurable by both parties
Review mechanism must be specified upfront (date, decision process)
Baseline schedule during trial period must be defined
domain_qualified.label: Phased trial structure with explicit success criteria

domain_qualified.feasibility_rationale: Parties identified 'exchange punctuality' as one of three main constraints. Party A needs control over exchange timing; Party B needs to demonstrate reliability. Tiered protocol addresses both: creates predictability Party A needs while giving Party B clear framework to build trust. Standard process option for custody cases with reliability concerns.
conditions: Specific notice windows to be set by parties (e.g., 30-min advance notice for delays)
Consequences for repeated late exchanges to be defined
Communication method for exchange coordination to be specified
Grace periods and emergency provisions to be negotiated
domain_qualified.label: Tiered exchange protocol with accountability mechanisms

domain_qualified.feasibility_rationale: Parties identified 'homework follow-through' as specific constraint. Party A concerned about routine disruption; Party B wants meaningful involvement in school-week activities (homework explicitly mentioned). Protocol addresses child's need for consistency while enabling Party B to participate actively in academic routine. Common tool in shared custody cases.
conditions: Parties must agree on communication platform/method
Homework handoff procedure to be specified (what information transfers, when, how)
Teacher communication access for both parents to be clarified
Expectations for each parent's homework support role to be defined
domain_qualified.label: Homework continuity protocol

domain_qualified.feasibility_rationale: Parties identified 'commute timing' as first of three main constraints. Party A concerned about reliability and child's routine; Party B's logistics capability is under question. A defined transportation system addresses the concrete concern while allowing Party B to demonstrate capability. May reveal whether transportation is genuine constraint or proxy for control issue.
conditions: Current transportation arrangements must be clarified (who does school drop-off/pickup now, what are the routes/times)
Party B's commute logistics must be specified (distance, flexibility, backup options)
School schedule and timing requirements must be established
Responsibility allocation for different scenarios (regular days, early dismissal, activities) to be negotiated
domain_qualified.label: Transportation coordination system

domain_qualified.feasibility_rationale: Party A willing to discuss trial 'if reliability conditions are explicit'; Party B accepts trial 'if it clearly leads to meaningful time.' Incremental approach bridges both needs: Party A gets proof-of-concept before major change, Party B gets guaranteed progression path if criteria are met. Addresses underlying trust gap while avoiding indefinite delay Party B fears.
conditions: Starting point schedule must be defined
Increment steps must be specified (e.g., one weeknight dinner → one overnight → two overnights)
Success criteria for each increment must be measurable
Timeline for checkpoints must be set (not open-ended)
Decision rule for progression must be clear (mutual agreement vs. objective criteria)
domain_qualified.label: Incremental expansion schedule with checkpoint reviews
